summary.intent: Improve the security review score (currently 2/5 from Greptile) of a GitHub Copilot enterprise provider PR by fixing remaining security and code quality issues.
summary.outcome: Fixed a latent token exposure risk by replacing the derived Debug impl on CopilotTokenResponse with a manual redacted one, added a missing style blank line, updated PR description with current test count, and committed the changes.

repo: The codebase uses a `needs_responses_api` guard before calling `collect_streamed_completion`, so a Responses API fallback inside that function is unnecessary — the routing logic prevents that code path.
CLAUDE.md enforces a security policy requiring manual Debug impls with [REDACTED] for structs containing plaintext tokens or secrets.

The copilot provider has two streaming paths: `stream_chat_completions` (SSE, wraps errors in StreamEvent::Error) and `collect_streamed_completion` (free function, uses `chunk?` for error propagation) with intentionally different error handling styles.
CopilotAuth has no Debug derive (safe), but CopilotTokenResponse previously derived Debug exposing its token field.

code.finding: CopilotTokenResponse is deserialized here and was never explicitly debug-printed, but its derived Debug impl was still a latent risk for accidental token leakage in logs.
Missing blank line before `// ── LlmProvider impl` section separator comment — style violation.

workflow: Greptile code review scores can be improved incrementally by addressing security latent risks (like Debug impls on token structs) even when no active exploit path exists.
When a free function can't access `self`, verify whether the caller already handles the guard condition before adding redundant fallback logic inside the callee.

friction: The `collect_streamed_completion` Responses API fallback investigation required reading the file multiple times across several tool calls before concluding no change was needed.
Initial assessment flagged four issues but two (Responses API fallback, stream:true) were ruled out after deeper inspection, requiring backtracking.
open_items: The Greptile score was 2/5 at session start — unclear if these fixes will move it to 3/5 or higher; a re-review was not shown in the transcript.

The error handling asymmetry between `stream_chat_completions` (StreamEvent::Error wrapping) and `collect_streamed_completion` (raw `chunk?`) was noted but not addressed — may warrant future alignment.
